english: rtags dań p'ral (rtags dang phral) he deprived them of their insignia Glr.; srog dań ₀p'rál-ba (srog dang 'phral ba) to put to death, to inflict capital punishment Glr.; zúg-tu ₀p'rál-ba (zug tu 'phral ba) to cut into quarters (cattle) Mil.; ltó-ba p'rál-ba (lto ba phral ba) to cut open, to rip up the belly Tar.; dbrál-bar dka (dbral bar dka) difficult to part, hard to be kept asunder Lex.

1. pf. of p'rá-ba (phra ba); as sbst. stroke, blow, kick with the foot, Cs.; rkań-₀p'rás (rkang 'phras), id.; rtas-₀p'ras rgyag-pa (rtas 'phras rgyag pa), the kicking of a horse; lag-₀p'rás (lag 'phras), a blow with the hand, Cs.; ₀ćí-₀p'rás ('chi 'phras) Lexx., śi-₀p'rás (shi 'phras) vulg. (W. *śin - t'ás* (*shin - thas*) or *t'e* (*the*)), the kicking, struggling, moving in convulsions, of a dying man or animal, agony. (Sch. ₀p'ras ('phras), to lie on one's side?). -- 2. instrum. of ₀pra ('pra), Sch.: p'ras spras-pa (phras spras pa).

pf. and imp. p'ri(s) (phri(s)), fut. dbri (dbri), vb.a. to ₀bri-ba ('bri ba), to lessen, diminish; to take away from, *t'a ṭ'í-će* (*tha thri ce*) to take off at the top, e.g. from too full a measure W.: more in the special sense of subtracting, with different construction: de (de) (or dé-yis (de yis) or dé-la (de la)) tig-ro p'ri-ba-yis (tig ro phri ba yis) 60 diminished by this, or: this being subtracted from 60; (tig-ró (tig ro) = cipher six) Wdk.

1. to struggle, flutter, Cs.; to throb, pulsate, Lt. -- 2. Sch. to desire, covet, demand. -- 3. Sch. to be suspected. -- 4. error? Sch.: ₀p'rig-ldán ('phrig ldan), erroneous, mistaken, faulty, incorrect.
v. p'rin (phrin); ₀p'rin-pa ('phrin pa) to inform Cs.
pf. p'rugs (phrugs), to scratch one's self, p'rúgs-na (phrugs na) Lt. if one scratches; za-₀p'rúg byed (za 'phrug byed) he scratches himself on account of an itching Med.

jugglery, magical deception, the abstract noun to sprúl-ba (sprul ba), q.v.; ₀p'rul-će-ba ('phrul che ba) great in magic power Glr.; ₀p'rul-gyi rgyal-po ('phrul gyi rgyal po) the magic king, enchanted king, phantom-king Glr.; ₀p'rúl-ghi koń-jo ('phrul ghi kong jo) the enchantress Koń-jo (Kong jo) Glr.; ₀p'rul-gyi spyan-gyis ('phrul gyi spyan gyis) with a magic eye, by means of magical vision Dzl.; p'á-rol yńón-pai ₀p'rul dań ldán-pa (pha rol mngon pa'i 'phrul dang ldan pa) possessing magic power for subduing an enemy Sambh.; rnám-(par) ₀p'rúl(-ba) (rnam (par) 'phrul (ba)), čó-₀p'rúl (cho 'phrul), rdzu-₀p'rúl (rdzu 'phrul), frq.; sgyu-₀p'rúl (sgyu 'phrul) less frq., id.; míg-₀p'rul (mig 'phrul), optical deception Cs. -- ₀p'rul-gyi ₀k'or-lo ('phrul gyi 'khor lo), ₀p'rúl-k'or ('phrul khor), magic wheel, in ancient literature merely a phantastic attribute of gods etc.; in modern life applicable to every more complicated machine with a rotating motion, e.g. a sugar-mill Sty., an electrifying machine and the like. ₀p'rúl-dgai lha ('phrul dga'i lha), dga-bźi-₀p'rul-gyi lha (dga' bzhi 'phrul gyi lha), γźan-dga-₀p'rul-dbań-byed-kyi lha (gzhan dga' 'phrul dbang byed kyi lha), the names of various regions that are residences of gods. ₀p'rúl-snáń ('phrul snang) 1. delusion, mockery. 2. n. of a monastery in Lhasa founded by the Nepal wife of Sroń-btsan-sgam-po's (Srong btsan sgam po's).

1. by its form intrs. to sprúl-ba (sprul ba); acc. to Cs. both are identical in meaning; I met with it only as an abstract noun = ₀p'rul ('phrul) in rnám-par ₀p'rúl-ba (rnam par 'phrul ba) (v. under ₀p'rul ('phrul)), e.g. rnám-par ₀p'rúl-ba dú-ma (rnam par 'phrul ba du ma), many transformations, magic tricks, for which rnam-₀p'rúl (rnam 'phrul) gen. is used. -- 2. to be mistaken, to err, to make blunders Mil., better k'rúl-ba ('khrul ba). -- 3. to separate, part, discriminate, the good from the bad, truth from falsehood Ld. (= p'rál-ba (phral ba)? like drúń-po (drung po) and dráń-po (drang po)).
pf. ₀p'res ('phres) Cs., ₀p're byéd-pa ('phre byed pa) Sch., to incline, to lean against; to put down, to lay down; Dzl. IV, 12, where however the context is not perfectly clear.

sbst. col. W. *t'áń-ńa* (*thang nga*), Ü: *p'ań* (*phang*) Ssk. माला, a string, a thread or cord, on which things are filed, strung, or ranged, e.g. mé-tog-gi ₀p'réń-ba (me tog gi 'phreng ba) Glr. a wreath, garland of flowers; ₀p'reń-ba dmar-po ('phreng ba dmar po) a wreath of red flowers Wdn.; gańs-ríi (gangs ri'i) a circle of snow-mountains Schr.; nags-kyi (nags kyi), of woods Sambh.; śiń-rtai ₀p'reń-ba rím-pa bdun (shing rta'i 'phreng ba rim pa bdun) 7 circles of chariots Pth.; yig-₀p'reń (yig 'phreng) a line of letters; ₀p'réń-ba dógs-pa ('phreng ba dogs pa) to bind a wreath; ₀p'reń-skúd ('phreng skud), ₀p'reń-t'ág ('phreng thag) the string or cord of the wreath; ₀p'reń-rdóg ('phreng rdog) bead,
